title: SQL: column references in JOIN
poc: CREATE TABLE test (i INT, id INT);
select * from test a inner join test b on (a.i = b.i);

select * from test a inner join test b on (a.i =
 b.i) inner join test c on (a.id = c.id);
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (i INT, id INT, value VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE test2 (j INT, id INT, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test1 (i, id, value) VALUES (1, 10, 'A'), (2, 20, 'B'), (NULL, 30, 'C');
INSERT INTO test2 (j, id, description) VALUES (1, 10, 'Desc1'), (2, NULL, 'Desc2'), (3, 20, 'Desc3');
SELECT * FROM test1 a INNER JOIN test2 b ON (a.id = b.id) WHERE a.value IS NOT NULL;
